title: BCom Accounting Salary 2026 | What Trainees & CAs Earn in SA

metaDescription: Thinking of studying Accounting? See the updated 2026 salary scales for BCom graduates in South Africa. From entry-level articles to qualified CA(SA) earnings in Gauteng vs. Cape Town.
content:
BCom Accounting Salary 2026: What You Can Really Earn in South Africa

Considering a BCom Accounting degree? One of the first questions students ask is: "What salary can I expect after graduating?" This guide breaks down the 2026 salary ranges for BCom Accounting graduates at every career stage — from trainee accountant to qualified CA(SA) — with regional comparisons between Gauteng and Cape Town.

Updated: February 27, 2026

BCom Accounting Salary Overview 2026

A BCom Accounting qualification opens doors to a wide range of finance and accounting careers in South Africa. Salaries vary significantly depending on:

  • Your level of experience (graduate, trainee, qualified)
  • Whether you are pursuing or have completed the CA(SA) route
  • Your industry (public sector, private sector, Big Four auditing firms)
  • Your location (Gauteng salaries typically exceed Western Cape by 10–20%)

Entry-Level BCom Accounting Salaries (2026)

Fresh BCom Accounting graduates entering the job market in 2026 can expect the following:

RoleGauteng (Annual)Cape Town (Annual)National Average
Junior BookkeeperR180,000–R240,000R165,000–R220,000R170,000–R230,000
Accounts ClerkR180,000–R250,000R165,000–R230,000R175,000–R240,000
Graduate Trainee (Big Four)R210,000–R280,000R200,000–R260,000R205,000–R270,000
Financial AdministratorR200,000–R270,000R185,000–R250,000R190,000–R260,000
Junior AuditorR220,000–R290,000R210,000–R275,000R215,000–R285,000

SAICA Trainee Accountant (Articles) Salary 2026

If you are pursuing the CA(SA) designation, you will complete a 3-year trainee accountant contract (articles) after your BCom Accounting degree and CTA/Honours. SAICA sets minimum stipends for trainee accountants:

Training YearBig Four Firms (Annual)Medium Firms (Annual)Small Firms (Annual)
Year 1 (Trainee)R220,000–R280,000R170,000–R220,000R130,000–R170,000
Year 2 (Trainee)R260,000–R320,000R200,000–R250,000R155,000–R195,000
Year 3 (Trainee)R300,000–R370,000R230,000–R290,000R175,000–R220,000

Note: Big Four firms (Deloitte, PwC, EY, KPMG) pay significantly more than smaller practices but also have higher performance expectations and longer hours.

Qualified CA(SA) Salary 2026

Once you pass the SAICA APC (Assessment of Professional Competence) and complete your articles, your earning potential jumps significantly:

RoleGauteng (Annual)Cape Town (Annual)Years of Experience
Newly Qualified CA(SA)R550,000–R750,000R500,000–R700,0000–2 years post-articles
CA(SA) — Financial ManagerR750,000–R1,100,000R680,000–R1,000,0003–6 years
CA(SA) — Finance DirectorR1,200,000–R2,000,000+R1,100,000–R1,800,000+7–12 years
CA(SA) — CFO (large company)R2,500,000–R5,000,000+R2,200,000–R4,500,000+12+ years

BCom Accounting Salaries Without CA(SA): Non-SAICA Route

Not everyone who studies BCom Accounting pursues the CA(SA) route. Many graduates build successful careers in accounting, finance, and management without completing articles. Here is what those career paths can earn:

RoleGauteng (Annual)Cape Town (Annual)Typical Experience
Accountant (SAIPA/non-CA)R300,000–R480,000R275,000–R440,0002–5 years
Management AccountantR380,000–R600,000R350,000–R560,0003–7 years
Internal AuditorR350,000–R550,000R320,000–R510,0002–6 years
Tax ConsultantR360,000–R580,000R330,000–R540,0003–6 years
Credit Analyst (banking)R320,000–R500,000R300,000–R470,0002–5 years
Finance ManagerR550,000–R850,000R500,000–R790,0005–10 years

Gauteng vs. Cape Town: Which City Pays More?

Gauteng (specifically Johannesburg and Pretoria) generally offers higher nominal salaries for accounting professionals than Cape Town. However, Cape Town's lower cost of living and lifestyle factors are attracting more finance talent:

  • Gauteng advantage: Higher nominal salaries, especially in corporate and Big Four firms headquartered in Sandton and Braamfontein
  • Cape Town advantage: Lower cost of living, growing tech-finance sector, increasing remote-work accounting opportunities
  • Salary gap: Gauteng accounting salaries are typically 10–20% higher than Cape Town equivalents at the same experience level

What Qualifications Boost Your BCom Accounting Salary?

Beyond the base BCom Accounting degree, these qualifications significantly increase earning potential:

  • CA(SA) — the gold standard in South African accounting. Salary premium: 50–150% over a basic BCom graduate
  • CIMA (Chartered Institute of Management Accountants) — highly valued in industry. Similar salary trajectory to CA(SA) in corporate roles
  • ACCA (Association of Chartered Certified Accountants) — recognised internationally and increasingly in South Africa
  • CFA (Chartered Financial Analyst) — ideal for investment banking, asset management. Very high earning potential in Gauteng
  • SAIPA (South African Institute of Professional Accountants) — more accessible than CA(SA), valued especially in SMEs

Is BCom Accounting a Good Career Choice in 2026?

Yes — accounting remains one of the most in-demand qualifications in South Africa. Key reasons:

  • High demand: Every business needs accountants — from startups to government departments
  • CA(SA) shortage: South Africa has a growing shortage of qualified Chartered Accountants, meaning competition for CA(SA) talent is fierce and salaries keep rising
  • Versatility: A BCom Accounting graduate can work in auditing, banking, taxation, financial management, consulting, or entrepreneurship
  • Remote work opportunities: Many accounting functions can now be done remotely, opening up international salary potential

Frequently Asked Questions About BCom Accounting Salaries
keywords: how much does a CA earn in South Africa
quickAnswer: BCom Accounting graduates in South Africa earn R180,000–R290,000 per year at entry level in 2026. Trainee accountants (articles) at Big Four firms earn R220,000–R370,000. Qualified CA(SA) professionals start at R550,000–R750,000, rising to R1M+ as Finance Managers. Gauteng salaries are typically 10–20% higher than Cape Town.

faqs.question: What is the starting salary for a BCom Accounting graduate in South Africa in 2026?
faqs.answer: A BCom Accounting graduate entering the South African job market in 2026 can expect a starting salary of R180,000–R280,000 per year, depending on the role and location. Entry-level positions include junior bookkeeper, accounts clerk, financial administrator, and trainee auditor. Graduates at Big Four firms (Deloitte, PwC, EY, KPMG) typically start at the higher end of the range.

faqs.question: How much do SAICA trainee accountants (articles) earn in 2026?
faqs.answer: SAICA trainee accountants doing their articles in 2026 earn between R130,000 and R370,000 per year depending on the firm size and training year. Big Four firms pay R220,000–R370,000, medium practices pay R170,000–R290,000, and smaller firms pay R130,000–R220,000. Stipends typically increase each year of the 3-year contract.

faqs.question: How much does a qualified CA(SA) earn in South Africa?
faqs.answer: A newly qualified CA(SA) in South Africa earns R550,000–R750,000 per year in 2026. With experience, CA(SA) professionals in Financial Manager roles earn R750,000–R1,100,000. Finance Directors earn R1.2M–R2M+, and CFOs at large companies can earn R2.5M–R5M+ per year. Gauteng-based CA(SA) professionals typically earn 10–20% more than those in Cape Town.

faqs.question: Is a BCom Accounting degree worth it in South Africa?
faqs.answer: Yes — BCom Accounting remains one of the most valuable degrees in South Africa in 2026. The CA(SA) pathway offers some of the highest earning potential in the country, and even without articles, BCom Accounting graduates are in demand across every sector. Accounting professionals also have strong job security, as every business requires financial management.

faqs.question: Do BCom Accounting salaries differ between Gauteng and Cape Town?
faqs.answer: Yes, Gauteng (especially Johannesburg and Sandton) typically offers 10–20% higher nominal salaries for accounting professionals compared to Cape Town. However, Cape Town's lower cost of living, growing fintech sector, and remote-work opportunities are narrowing the gap. Many Cape Town accountants supplement local salaries with remote work for Johannesburg-based or international clients.
